published_prior_art.cite: TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch and LeeGhassamiShpitser2024DataFusionSelection give general multi-source identification algorithms; no closed source-cover certificate or overlap-hedge witness.

LeeGhassamiShpitser2024DataFusionSelection provides a general identification algorithm for data fusion under systematic selection; the proposal's remaining gap is a restricted polynomial criterion plus explicit overlap-hedge witness.
ShpitserPearl2008CompleteID and CorreaTianBareinboim2018Adjustment cover the ordinary-ID and adjustment corners; the source-cover collapse is bookkeeping.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser 2024, UAI, 'A General Identification Algorithm For Data Fusion Problems Under Systematic Selection', which is closer than DoSearch/Bareinboim-Pearl for multiple systematically selected data sources; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the epsilon-gap is sharpened against this algorithm.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add LeeGhassamiShpitser2024DataFusionSelection to Related work, Bibliography, and the Conjecture 1 why-new block, then state exactly whether the source-cover class is outside SS-ID, a polynomial-time specialization of it, or a witness-completeness refinement.

Upgrade the flagship claim from 'closed-form criterion' prose to a formal separation: specify the observable input subclass, the complexity of Cover, and the output that SS-ID/do-search does not provide, such as a canonical finite overlap-hedge pair with preserved source laws.
Make the three-variable, three-source boundary example in the proof sketch into an explicit sanity corollary or worked witness template so the overlap-hedge object is not only named abstractly.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison around Lee-Ghassami-Shpitser 2024 and sharpen the kernel into a demonstrable flagship-level refinement rather than a narrower restatement of general data-fusion identification.
